title: ASAN: High debug levels trigger stack-use-after-scope
body:
I have built Verilator with AddressSanitizer enabled, and encountered a bug as follows:

```cpp
==227500==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: stack-use-after-scope on address 0x7ffd6eaa0b50 at pc 0x0000004bec58 bp 0x7ffd6eaa0460 sp 0x7ffd6eaa0458
READ of size 8 at 0x7ffd6eaa0b50 thread T0
    #0 0x4bec57 in AstNode::dumpPtrs(std::ostream&) const ../V3Ast.cpp:1112
    #1 0x4bf5a1 in AstNode::dumpTree(std::ostream&, std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, int) const ../V3Ast.cpp:1122
    #2 0x4bf21e in AstNode::dumpTree(std::ostream&, std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, int) const ../V3Ast.cpp:1132
    #3 0x4bf170 in AstNode::dumpTree(std::ostream&, std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, int) const ../V3Ast.cpp:1129
    #4 0x4c637f in AstNode::dumpTreeFile(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, bool, bool, bool) ../V3Ast.cpp:1163
    #5 0xa8319e in V3Global::dumpCheckGlobalTree(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, int, bool) ../V3Global.cpp:96
    #6 0xc4dc32 in V3LinkLevel::modSortByLevel() ../V3LinkLevel.cpp:81
    #7 0x441d86 in process ../Verilator.cpp:117                                                                                                               
    #8 0x441d86 in verilate ../Verilator.cpp:606                                                                                                              
    #9 0x441d86 in main ../Verilator.cpp:732     
    #10 0x7efe102dad09 in __libc_start_main ../csu/libc-start.c:308
    #11 0x44c2d9 in _start (/home/ant/src/verilator-1/bin/verilator_bin+0x44c2d9)
```

The relevant source code reads:
```cpp
    if (m_iterpp) {                              // 1110
        os << " iterpp=" << cvtToHex(m_iterpp);  // 1111
        os << "*=" << cvtToHex(*m_iterpp);       // 1112
    }                                            // 1113
```

I guess that the code here might be the cause (since niterp is a local scoped variable), but I am not sure of how it could be fixed, because it looks correct to me:
```cpp
        niterp->m_iterpp = &niterp;
        niterp->accept(v);
        // accept may do a replaceNode and change niterp on us...
        // niterp maybe nullptr, so need cast if printing
        // if (niterp != nodep) UINFO(1,"iterateAndNext edited "<<cvtToHex(nodep)
        //                             <<" now into "<<cvtToHex(niterp)<<endl);
        if (!niterp) return;  // Perhaps node deleted inside accept
        niterp->m_iterpp = nullptr;
```


Steps to reproduce (the test chosen is not much relevant, it happens with almost every testcase):
```sh
export CFLAGS=-fsanitize=address CXXFLAGS=-fsanitize=address LDFLAGS=-fsanitize=address
autoconf
./configure
make -j`nproc`
test_regress/t/t_interface_inl.pl --debugi 9
```
You can also set `ASAN_OPTIONS=color=always` to get a colourful output.

I believe it did not crash without ASAN, because all the erroneous code does is read a no-longer-valid address, and print it, without dereferencing.  It is nevertheless limiting, because in order to use ASAN to debug my contributions to Verilator, I needed to restrict the debug level to 8, so I could not see the intermediate trees while using ASAN.
